Headline: Something is rising on the Gulfport waterfront.
Copy:
Something is rising on the Gulfport waterfront.

A $105M district at 25th & West Beach — 136 residences, retail, dining, and Hotel Holden, all facing the water.

Channel South. Downtown Gulfport.

Headline: Built where the coast began.
Copy:
Built where the coast began.

Channel South rises on the site of the Great Southern Hotel — the 1903 landmark that first drew the world to Gulfport's waterfront.

Same ground. New chapter.
Notes: Story: poll 'A telephone in every room — guess the year' → reveal 1903.

Headline: Meet Hotel Holden.
Copy:
Meet Hotel Holden.

A 114-room Marriott Tribute hotel at the heart of Channel South — with a rooftop over Jones Park and the Harbor.

More to reveal.

Interested in leasing or retail? DM us or visit channelsouth.com.
Notes: Story: Holden 'tap to reveal' 3-frame + follow nudge.

Headline: Sunset over the Harbor, from a rooftop that doesn't exist yet.
Copy:
Sunset over the Harbor, from a rooftop that doesn't exist yet.

Hotel Holden's rooftop will look out over Jones Park and the water.

Coming to downtown Gulfport.
Visual Specs: Photoreal rooftop-bar-sunset + Holden rooftop render (slow push-in)
Notes: Story: leasing link sticker on a residence render.

Headline: From the ground up.
Copy:
From the ground up.

Construction began in December. The parking structure rises first — then residences and Hotel Holden through 2027.

Channel South is moving.
Visual Specs: ⚠️ GAP: real construction photo. Interim: parking deck aerial → corner-aerial-golden (then/now)

Headline: Did you know? The Great Southern Hotel had a telephone in every room — in 1903.
Copy:
Did you know? The Great Southern Hotel had a telephone in every room, hot and cold running water, and its own power plant — in 1903.

Gulfport's waterfront has always been ahead of its time.
Visual Specs: Heritage archival set, close on establishing render (6 slides)

Headline: Rooted in story.
Copy:
Rooted in story.

Hotel Holden is designed around Gulfport's coastal legacy — atmosphere, texture, experience. Interiors by Flick Mars.

A first look is coming.

Leasing + retail → channelsouth.com.

Headline: Meet the district.
Copy:
Meet the district.

17,637 sq ft of curated retail and dining, pedestrian streets, and public space — all on the Gulfport waterfront.

This is Channel South.

Interested in a space? DM us.

Headline: 294 spaces first. Then the skyline.
Copy:
294 spaces first. Then the skyline.

The parking structure is the first piece of Channel South to rise — delivering this year.
Visual Specs: ⚠️ GAP: construction photo. Interim: parking deck aerial

Headline: Meet the man who made the port.
Copy:
Meet the man who made the port.

Captain Joseph T. Jones dredged the channel that created the Port of Gulfport — and built the Great Southern Hotel on this very ground.

Channel South rises on his legacy.
Visual Specs: ⚠️ GAP: Capt. Jones portrait/statue (find public-domain). Interim: heritage + harbor archival (6 slides)

Headline: The names will come from Gulfport itself.
Copy:
The names will come from Gulfport itself.

Hotel Holden's dining and rooftop will draw their names from the coast's own maritime history — its founders, its harbor, its shipwrights.

The stories are being chosen now.
Visual Specs: Holden entry render + brass/maritime texture + heritage (keep outlet names internal)
Notes: Story: 'tap to reveal the inspiration' (harbor / shipwright / crane).

Headline: A waterfront for the long weekend.
Copy:
A waterfront for the long weekend.

Picture the Fourth on the Gulfport waterfront — the water, the harbor, the rise of something new.

Channel South is on its way.
Notes: Story: this-or-that 'rooftop sunset or harbor morning?'

Headline: On schedule.
Copy:
On schedule.

Residences and Hotel Holden deliver through 2027 — and every week gets us closer.

Channel South, downtown Gulfport.
